ticket_title: Specify Route Registry, Shell and Page-Job Contract
purpose: Make route registry, shell and page-job decisions once in shared contracts so later domain screens cannot re-solve process ownership, page job, slot placement or no-overclaim behavior locally.

controlled_vocabulary.route_contract: Every screen route must expose page id, path, route scope, role/workflow metadata and client visibility sensitivity from the route registry or a derived typed policy.
controlled_vocabulary.shell_contract: Every productive process-first page must render through a shared shell or approved shared renderer that exposes process zones and no-overclaim metadata through typed props and runtime data attributes.
controlled_vocabulary.page_job_contract: Every route has one primary page job that controls zones, command placement, proof/audit placement, freeform children policy and allowed renderer family.
controlled_vocabulary.runtime_contract: Tests must be able to inspect page job, active step, template family, route id, worksurface id, zone placement and protected-route behavior without interpreting visual copy.

allowed_usage: Derive page-job, active-step, workspace, density, proof-posture and no-overclaim metadata from route registry plus shared UX policy.
Use shared renderers such as WorksurfaceShell, UxHubPage, UxDetailStandardPanel, RouteSkeletonPage and PageTemplateFrame.
Expose runtime data attributes for route id, page job, active step, template family, action-zone behavior, proof/audit placement and classified children.
Classify child content into approved shell zones before rendering it on productive P0 routes.
Keep reference, hold and P1 routes mechanically non-productive unless a later explicit unlock decision supplies route authority and tests.
Add focused source or Playwright tests that assert the shared contract instead of route-local visual assumptions.
forbidden_usage: Do not let a domain screen infer its own page job outside the shared policy layer.
Do not use a status chip, visual screenshot, route presence or visible button as process completion proof.
Do not render unclassified freeform children on productive P0 routes.
Do not create a second primary command zone through summary rails, side panels, sticky blocks or page-local action groups.
Do not make protected, held, P1 or reference routes count as productive MVP completion proof.
Do not expose route ids, page ids or internal debug labels on client-safe surfaces as visible product copy.
Do not create routes, schemas, APIs, migrations, image assets, screen assets or state-screen assets as part of this shared contract implementation.

acceptance_rules: Every registered screen route must produce exactly one page-template record.
Duplicate page IDs, missing template records or productive templates on protected routes fail the contract.
Client-sensitive routes must derive client-safe visibility behavior from shared visibility policy, not local copy.
Route policy may group existing routes, but route creation or split still requires True-UX route-evolution authority.

command_zone_rules: A productive page may have exactly one primary command zone.
Disabled or blocked commands must name the missing condition without implying downstream completion.
Client visibility, advisor approval, compliance release, evidence sufficiency and export delivery remain separate command meanings.
children_rules: Productive routes require classified children only.
Reference and hold routes may render reference context but may not expose mutation slots.
Proof/audit children must map to proof_audit_zone or approved secondary context, never to uncontrolled vertical appendices.

representative_route_expectations.no_overclaim_focus: Client context and released visibility stay separated.
Signal review is internal orientation, not advisor approval or release.
Compliance decision room separates release, block, audit and client visibility.
Export creation/scope does not approve, generate, download or share.
Reference pages are not productive MVP workflow proof.

no_overclaim_rules: Upload success is not evidence sufficiency.
Advisor approval is not compliance release.
Compliance release is not client acceptance.
Export preview is not approval, generation, download or share.
Admin visibility is not admin bypass authority.
Client context is not released client projection.
Reference, hold and P1 routes are not productive implementation proof.
implementation_acceptance_criteria: Shared types expose the route, shell and page-job contract without route-local redefinition.
Runtime data attributes are present on at least one representative adopted consumer and remain hidden as product copy.
Source tests assert required attributes, page-job vocabulary, protected-route behavior and representative route expectations.
At least one representative consumer uses the contract without adding a route-local page-job map.
No route, schema, API, image, screen asset or state-screen asset is created for this contract slice.

recommended_implementation_sequence.action: Add or extend typed source-level contract and runtime data attributes.
recommended_implementation_sequence.minimum_proof: Source tests assert the new contract attributes.

recommended_implementation_sequence.action: Migrate one severe or representative consumer to consume the shared contract directly.
recommended_implementation_sequence.minimum_proof: Targeted route/source test proves the representative route uses the shared primitive.

recommended_implementation_sequence.action: Add regression tests for no-overclaim and required slots.
recommended_implementation_sequence.minimum_proof: Tests prove old unclassified stacking and forbidden page-job drift cannot return.

recommended_implementation_sequence.action: Run targeted source and screenshot or route proof for the shared contract.
recommended_implementation_sequence.minimum_proof: Contract tests plus route/screenshot proof if a visible consumer changed.

human_decision_required_if: implementation needs a new route, route split or route deprecation
implementation would promote a protected route into productive MVP navigation
implementation cannot satisfy the contract without a visible product-scope change

definition_of_done_check.screenshot_reason: Specification-only ticket with no visible UI change.
